rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Choi and Bell (2011) to introduce a "preference minorities" mechanism for why social earned media may precede and stimulate traditional earned media. It then returns to that mechanism in the discussion to interpret why social channels may work better for a niche offering like Kiva. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because the cited work provides a named conceptual lens that informs the paper's framing and interpretation, even though it is not directly re-estimated or extended methodologically.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the preference-minority concept from Choi and Bell (2011) to explain channel dynamics in earned media, arguing that niche-interest consumers are better reached in social outlets and can eventually attract traditional media attention.
